title: Docker for Java Applications
learningObjectives: Explain **Docker for Java** with production trade-offs, SLO thinking, and failure modes
Implement small **Java** snippets that anchor interview whiteboard discussions
Connect **observability** and **capacity** signals to real operational decisions
Identify common **pitfalls** that cause incidents or cost surprises at scale
Compare alternatives using **constraints** (latency, cost, team skills, compliance)
Present answers with **structure**: problem, options, decision, verification

sections.title: Why Docker for Java Applications matters
sections.content: Containers package **your** **JVM** assumptions. Interviewers want **multi-stage** builds, **non-root**, and **resource** awareness—not a fat image that happens to run.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Docker for Java Applications
sections.content:
### Day 72 — **Docker** for **Java**

### 1. Images
**Layers**, **caching**, **pinning** digests.

### 2. Multi-stage
**Build** vs **runtime** separation.

### 3. JVM in containers
**Memory** and **CPU** flags vs **cgroups**.

### 4. Security
**Non-root**, **read-only** rootfs where possible.

### 5. Debugging
**distroless** trade-offs.

### 6. Story
**90%** smaller image after multi-stage + **jlink** discussion for specialized runtimes.

sections.title: Basic — Container vocabulary
sections.output:
image: immutable template
container: running instance with writable layer

sections.title: Intermediate — Layer order principle
sections.output: copy pom before source for better docker layer cache

sections.title: Advanced — Multi-stage headline
sections.output: multi-stage: build JDK image -> ship slim JRE runtime only

sections.title: Multi-stage build
sections.description: Builder produces JAR; runtime copies artifact.
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 72 — Multi-stage
participant Builder
participant Runtime
Builder -> Builder : mvn package
Builder -> Runtime : copy jar
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Docker for Java Applications (Day 72 assignment)
sections.problem:
Print **exactly** two lines:
1. Implemented: Docker for Java Applications
2. Focus: Multi-stage Dockerfile for Spring Boot JAR.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: Why **multi-stage** **build** for **Spring** **Boot**?
conceptual.answer: Compile in builder image; ship slim runtime with only JAR—smaller attack surface. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Docker for Java**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Compile in builder image; ship slim runtime with only JAR—smaller attack surface. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Compile in builder image; ship slim runtime with only JAR—smaller attack surface. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.question: **distroless** vs **slim** **JRE** images?
conceptual.answer: Less shell tooling vs easier debugging—trade-off per environment. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Less shell tooling vs easier debugging—trade-off per environment. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Docker for Java Applications** deploy correlates with **5xx** spike.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze risky changes; open **SLO** dashboard.

**(2) Root cause** **Config** regression or **dependency** **saturation**.

**(3) Fix** Rollback or **toggle** **flag**; restore **SLO** then root-cause.

**(4) Prevention** Add **canary** gate and **diff** **alerts** on **golden** metrics. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: How do you **communicate** **impact** and **ETA** during this incident?
followUps.answer: Share **SLO** **burn**, **customer** **regions** affected, **mitigation** steps in flight, and **explicit** unknowns. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.answer: At minimum: **runbook** update, **dashboard** gap closed, **test** that **reproduces** the failure, and **owner** with **deadline**. Tie themes to **error** **budget** policy. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Recall |
|---|---|
| Multi-stage | Slim runtime |
| Layer cache | Order matters |
| Non-root | USER directive |
| Pin digest | Reproducible |
| cgroup | JVM ergonomics |
| distroless | Small attack surface |
| .dockerignore | Context size |
| HEALTHCHECK | Probe alignment |
| ENTRYPOINT | Exec form |
| jlink | Custom JRE |
